ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — IIS w3wp.exe drops .aspx web shell into Exchange virtual directories (ProxyLogon / China Chopper)
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical HAFNIUM / Operation Exchange Marauder post-exploit step where the on-prem Exchange IIS worker process (w3wp.exe), abused via the ProxyLogon SSRF→RCE chain (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065), writes a China Chopper or similar .aspx/.ashx/.asmx web shell into OWA/ECP auth directories or aspnet_client. This pattern is overwhelmingly tied to HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon and copy-cat clusters (CrowdStrike: ETHEREAL PANDA-adjacent; Microsoft: Silk Typhoon). w3wp writing executable handler files into Exchange auth paths is virtually never benign.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_paths values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hashes values(Filesystem.user) as users from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\Owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*") by host Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.action | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename host as dest | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe") by host Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.user Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest]
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — Exchange ProxyLogon web-shell drop
// Stage A: w3wp.exe writing .aspx/.ashx/.asmx into Exchange auth paths (China Chopper signature)
let ExchangePaths = dynamic([
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"]);
let ShellWrites =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
    | where FolderPath has_any (ExchangePaths)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
              InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
// Stage B: w3wp.exe spawning OS recon / cred-access children — China Chopper "oneliner" execution
let ShellExec =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe",
                          "nltest.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe",
                          "systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe",
                          "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","7z.exe","rar.exe","makecab.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName,
              ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
union ShellWrites, ShellExec
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — Exchange ProxyLogon web-shell drop (Sentinel ASIM)
let ExchangePaths = dynamic([
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"]);
let ShellWrites =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where ActingProcessName has "w3wp.exe"
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asmx"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (ExchangePaths)
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, TargetFileName, TargetFilePath,
              TargetFileSHA256, ActingProcessCommandLine, Stage="WebShellWrite";
let ShellExec =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where ParentProcessName has "w3wp.exe"
    | where TargetProcessName has_any ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe",
          "nltest.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","systeminfo.exe",
          "tasklist.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe",
          "rundll32.exe","7z.exe","rar.exe","makecab.exe")
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessCommandLine,
              Stage="w3wpChildExec";
union ShellWrites, ShellExec
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Exchange Web Shell Drop by w3wp.exe
id: 8e6c5d1a-2b9f-4d7e-a3c1-9f8b5e1a2c3d
status: stable
description: Detects ProxyLogon / Operation Exchange Marauder webshell drops where IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) writes .aspx/.ashx/.asmx files into Exchange OWA/ECP auth directories or aspnet_client — a tradecraft signature of Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM).
references:
    - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
    - attack.persistence
    - attack.t1190
    - attack.t1505.003
    - detection.emerging_threats
    - g0125
logsource:
    product: windows
    category: file_event
detection:
    selection_proc:
        Image|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    selection_ext:
        TargetFilename|endswith:
            - '.aspx'
            - '.ashx'
            - '.asmx'
    selection_path:
        TargetFilename|contains:
            - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\'
            - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\'
            - '\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\'
            - '\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\'
            - '\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\'
    condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
    - Exchange Cumulative Update install (rare; correlate with msiexec/setup.exe parent and Microsoft-signed file hash)
level: critical
ucs.rationale: This is THE signature post-exploit pattern of Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM. Microsoft, Volexity and Mandiant all attribute the original ProxyLogon mass-exploitation wave (Operation Exchange Marauder, March 2021) to HAFNIUM, with China Chopper .aspx web shells dropped by SYSTEM-context w3wp.exe into the OWA/ECP/aspnet_client paths in this query. Patches don't make the artefact go away — late-patching ten
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — LSASS dump via procdump or comsvcs.dll on Exchange / IIS hosts
ucs.description: Hunts the credential-access stage Silk Typhoon performs immediately after a successful Exchange web-shell foothold: dumping LSASS with procdump.exe (their documented tool of choice) or the rundll32 + comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBAS technique, often launched from w3wp.exe lineage or by SYSTEM-context shells on Exchange / IIS / SharePoint servers. This catches HAFNIUM's transition from initial access to lateral movement / domain compromise.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents values(Processes.user) as users from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ( (Processes.process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") OR Processes.original_file_name="procdump") AND Processes.process="*lsass*" ) OR ( Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" AND Processes.process="*MiniDump*" ) OR ( Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*MiniDumpWriteDump*" OR Processes.process="*Out-Minidump*") ) by host Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — LSASS credential dump on Exchange / IIS host
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
   or InitiatingProcessAccountName =~ "system"     // HAFNIUM dumps as SYSTEM via w3wp lineage
| where
    // Procdump targeting LSASS (HAFNIUM's documented tool)
    ( (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
         or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName =~ "procdump"
         or ProcessVersionInfoInternalFileName =~ "procdump")
      and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass","-ma ","-mp ") )
   // comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBAS (rundll32 C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <pid> <file> full)
   or ( FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
        and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs"
        and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump" )
   // PowerShell in-process LSASS dump
   or ( FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
        and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDumpWriteDump","Out-Minidump","Invoke-Mimikatz","sekurlsa::") )
| extend ParentChain = strcat(InitiatingProcessParentFileName, " -> ", InitiatingProcessFileName, " -> ", FileName)
| extend IsExchangeContext = InitiatingProcessFileName has_any ("w3wp.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe")
      and (InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "w3wp.exe" or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\Exchange Server\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentChain, IsExchangeContext,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — LSASS credential dump on Exchange / IIS host (Sentinel)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" or SubjectUserName =~ "SYSTEM"
| where
    // Procdump on LSASS
    ( (NewProcessName endswith "\\procdump.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\procdump64.exe")
      and (CommandLine has "lsass" or CommandLine has " -ma " or CommandLine has " -mp ") )
   // rundll32 + comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBAS
   or ( NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
        and CommandLine has "comsvcs"
        and CommandLine has "MiniDump" )
   // PowerShell in-memory LSASS dump
   or ( (NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe")
        and (CommandLine has "MiniDumpWriteDump" or CommandLine has "Out-Minidump"
             or CommandLine has "Invoke-Mimikatz" or CommandLine has "sekurlsa::") )
| extend IsExchangeLineage = ParentProcessName has_any ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe","EdgeTransport.exe")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, IsExchangeLineage
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) LSASS Credential Dump via procdump or comsvcs MiniDump
id: 4f3b7a92-1c8d-4e5b-b6a2-7d9c2e4f8a1b
status: stable
description: Detects LSASS process memory dumping via procdump.exe (HAFNIUM tool of choice), rundll32+comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBAS, or PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump — the canonical credential-access step Silk Typhoon performs after Exchange ProxyLogon foothold.
references:
    - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
    - attack.credential_access
    - attack.t1003.001
    - attack.t1218.011
    - g0125
logsource:
    product: windows
    category: process_creation
detection:
    procdump_lsass:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\procdump.exe'
            - '\procdump64.exe'
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'lsass'
            - ' -ma '
            - ' -mp '
    procdump_renamed:
        OriginalFileName: 'procdump'
        CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
    comsvcs_minidump:
        Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
        CommandLine|contains|all:
            - 'comsvcs'
            - 'MiniDump'
    pwsh_minidump:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '\pwsh.exe'
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'MiniDumpWriteDump'
            - 'Out-Minidump'
            - 'sekurlsa::'
            - 'Invoke-Mimikatz'
    condition: 1 of procdump_lsass or procdump_renamed or comsvcs_minidump or pwsh_minidump
falsepositives:
    - Authorised IR teams collecting LSASS dumps for forensics (correlate with change ticket / approved-host watchlist)
level: critical
ucs.rationale: Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Volexity both documented that HAFNIUM operators followed up the ProxyLogon web-shell drop by deploying procdump.exe to dump LSASS process memory and exfiltrate it for offline credential extraction. Procdump-with-LSASS is the single most attributed Silk Typhoon credential-access TTP (their MITRE technique list shows T1003.001 and T1003 prominently). Including the c
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.
